title: shinylive
text:
Shinylive is a way to run Shiny applications directly in a web browser without needing a server.


Traditional Shiny: - Requires a server - Runs R in the backend
Shinylive: - Runs entirely in the browser - No server required




Easy deployment
No hosting cost
Good for simple apps





Limited functionality
Not suitable for large datasets
Slower initial load




Shinylive is useful for sharing simple interactive dashboards, while traditional Shiny is better for more complex applications.

title: Shiny
text:
This page presents my interactive dashboard built using Shiny.


Unlike the static dashboard, this app allows users to explore the data dynamically by selecting different filters and metrics.




Filter by date range

Select different performance metrics

Compare content types

Explore trends over time




[Launch Shiny App](https://eunicew.shinyapps.io/msdm-project-eunice/



This interactive approach allows users to analyze performance from multiple perspectives instead of relying on a single summary.

title: Dashboard
text:
This page showcases my static dashboard built using Quarto.


The dashboard analyzes ODI Grips’ Instagram performance to understand which content types perform best.



View Full Dashboard)



Content types such as sales and educational posts tend to generate stronger engagement compared to others.

title: Index
text:
I am a graduate student in the Master of Science in Digital Marketing program at Cal Poly Pomona. I am interested in digital marketing and using data to guide business decisions.



My goal is to work in a role where I can combine data analysis with marketing strategy to improve performance and drive results.
